name: Kestria Global Conference 2026
tagline: Leading with Authenticity. Building with Trust.

keynotes.name: Norman Chua
keynotes.title: Founder
keynotes.bio:
An experienced HR leader and coach, Norman has over 20 years of corporate experiences spanning across public and private sectors. Often known as a “HR who is non-HR”, Norman has brought a breath of freshness to the space of people development and organisation culture.

In his corporate roles, Norman was often driving changes within organisations, leading teams through transformation initiatives and advocating cultural shifts.

A strong public speaker, Norman was invited as keynote speaker in various forums such as Adult Learning Symposium in 2010, HKMA (Hong Kong Management Association) Seminar in Hong Kong in 2011, HRM Asia seminar in 2014 and Jobstreet Career Fair 2023. He has also shared his inspirations through social and public media, guest speaking and podcasting on various radio stations in Singapore such as 938LIVE, KISS92, LOVE972, Capia958 and CNA, on topics ranging from organisation culture, leadership, and personal development.

His versatility of experiences and languages enable Norman to better embrace differences in culture and build strong relationships with stakeholders. Leveraging on this strength, Norman has designed and delivered development initiatives across North America, Europe and Asia, for audiences of diverse nationalities and seniorities. His deep insights and anecdotal sharing often leave audiences with much reflection and inspiration.

keynotes.name: Steven B. McKinney
keynotes.title: Founder & CEO
keynotes.bio:
Steve McKinney has twenty-five years of experience leading professional services in Asia and over twelve years of leadership at global athletic footwear companies Adidas, Reebok, and Converse. In 2001, he established McKinney Consulting, an executive search and leadership consulting firm based in Seoul. McKinney Consulting is also a partner firm for Kestria, the world’s largest executive search alliance.

Steve has coached hundreds of CEOs for start-up companies and leaders of Fortune 500 and other multinational companies, and he is a keynote speaker focusing on proactive agility.

keynotes.name: Dr. Ayesha Khanna
keynotes.title: CEO
keynotes.bio:
Dr. Ayesha Khanna is CEO of Addo AI and one of the world’s leading voices on artificial intelligence — not as theory, but as a hands-on tool for transformation. Over two decades, she has helped Fortune 500 companies and governments operationalize AI at scale, with clients including Johnson & Johnson, Visa, Pfizer, and Mastercard.

She serves on the boards of Johnson Controls (New York Stock Exchange) and NEOM Tonomus in the Middle East, and previously served on the Global Scientific Advisory Board of L’Oréal. Reuters named her one of the world’s Trailblazing Women in Enterprise AI (2026), she was named to Edelman’s Top 50 AI Creators (2025) and Forbes featured her as one of Southeast Asia’s groundbreaking entrepreneurs.

A graduate of Harvard, Columbia, and the London School of Economics, Dr. Khanna reaches more than 300,000 leaders and decision-makers across her platforms and newsletters. Her work has been featured in The New York Times, TIME, Harvard Business Review, and Forbes.

partners.name: Joseph Blass
partners.title: CEO
partners.bio: Joseph started his career as an economist in the public sector. He then worked for a global family business managing a varied portfolio of investments in manufacturing, real estate and financial services. Prior to founding Ezekia, Joseph was an executive, managing both privately held and publicly listed Telecom and IT Companies. He founded Ezekia in 2015 and, together with his team, has built Ezekia into the leading CRM and Assignment Management tool for executive search firms. Joseph holds a Master’s in Economics and is a (very) amateur artist.
partners.companyDescription: About Ezekia: Ezekia is a full-stack CRM, business development and assignment management platform for executive search firms. It is easy to use, customisable to your own work patterns and offers an excellent user experience. Besides superb reporting tools (both for clients and for management), it includes a client portal, a billing module, built-in AI and various APIs enabling numerous integrations. Migrating data from another platform onto Ezekia is quick and simple. All training and support are free and clients are not tied into long-term contracts. Ezekia is ISO 27001 certified, employs 60 people and has thousands of users across the globe.

partners.name: Krista Pedersen
partners.title: Managing Director, Asia Pacific
partners.bio:
Based in China, Krista Pedersen is Hogan’s Managing Director of Asia Pacific. With advanced fluency in Chinese and nearly two decades of international business experience, Krista is focused on growing and accelerating Hogan’s brand and business in Asia. She oversees 14 markets in Asia and manages a team that supports Hogan’s 12 distributors in the region with thought leadership, client development, product support, and solutions implementation.

Krista has represented Hogan at conferences and events, speaking at the Association of Test Publishers (ATP) Innovations in Testing Conference, the International Association for Cross-Cultural Psychology (IACCP) Conference in Japan, the DDI Conference on Assessment Center Methods in Shanghai, and many additional distributor and client events each year. With her cross-cultural experience, Krista has also worked with Hogan’s Data Science team to translate and culturally adapt select Hogan reports and assessments. She recently co-designed and initiated Hogan’s Global Leader Effectiveness Study (GLES), a survey that measures leader effectiveness by asking followers about their ideal leaders.

From 2024-2025 Krista was the Chair of the Asia Association of Test Publishers (AATP) Steering Board. Krista is a PhD candidate in Organizational Development and Change (ODC) at Fielding Graduate University. She received her Master’s degree in ODC from Fielding and graduated Magna Cum Laude from Baylor University.

partners.name: Nicole M. Dickie
partners.title: Senior Consultant, Asia Pacific
partners.bio:
Nicole Dickie, MA is a Senior Consultant at Hogan Assessments, where she concentrates on growing Hogan’s brand across Asia Pacific. Based in Taipei, she plays a key role in expanding Hogan’s presence across Greater China, Korea, and Southeast Asia, partnering with distributors and solutions partners to support sustainable business growth.

Nicole is an Industrial-Organizational Psychologist with expertise spanning leadership development, succession planning, employee engagement, executive assessment, and organizational change. Her work focuses on translating rigorous psychological research into practical, culturally relevant solutions that align with organizational strategy, values, and long-term performance goals. She has contributed to global thought leadership on cross-cultural leadership and talent development through articles, podcasts, and international conference presentations.

Nicole also co-led Hogan’s Global Leadership Effectiveness Study, examining perceptions of ideal leadership across countries and cultures. The research has gathered insights from more than 10,000 respondents across 120 countries. Prior to joining Hogan, Nicole worked as a management consultant and corporate trainer, delivering leadership training and coaching for organizations such as TSMC, PwC, and Aveda. Nicole holds a Master’s degree in Industrial-Organizational Psychology from the University of Tulsa.

partners.name: Anne-Marie Paiement, PhD
partners.title: Regional Manager, Asia Pacific
partners.bio:
Anne-Marie Paiement, PhD, is the Regional Manager, Asia Pacific on the international distributors team at Hogan Assessments. Based in Australia, Anne-Marie concentrates on expanding Hogan’s brand and facilitating business growth in the Asia-Pacific region. She also serves as a subject matter expert and contributes to global discussions on cross-cultural leadership, diversity and inclusion, talent development, and psychometric assessments. Her work has taken her across many continents and countries, including Australia, Canada, Indonesia, Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, Singapore, Thailand, and the US.

Prior to joining Hogan, Anne-Marie gained consulting, academic teaching, and conferencing experiences. As a consultant, she designed and implemented global talent management initiatives to promote leadership development, cross-cultural team acceleration, and career progression. She has administered nearly 300 psychometric assessments with feedback for hiring and development purposes. As a lecturer, she taught IO psychology and organizational behavior. As a researcher, she presented her research on leadership at national and international conferences.

Anne-Marie holds a Doctor of Philosophy in Industrial-Organizational Psychology from the University of Montreal, Canada. She is a registered psychologist in Canada (OPQ) and Australia (AHPRA).

travelTips.title: Airport to hotel
travelTips.body: Changi Airport (SIN) to Andaz Singapore (Bugis MRT): Grab/taxi ~S$25–35 (25–35 min). MRT Downtown Line from T2/T3 direct to Bugis ~S$2.50. Changi Express on ride-hailing apps is also available.

travelTips.title: Currency
travelTips.body: Singapore Dollar (SGD). Cards are accepted almost everywhere; contactless (Apple/Google Pay) and PayNow are widely used. No tipping culture.

travelTips.title: Cash
travelTips.body: Small notes handy for hawker centres, coffee shops and older taxis. ATMs are plentiful; many accept foreign cards.

travelTips.title: Power adapter
travelTips.body: Type G plugs (UK-style, 3 pins), 230V/50Hz. Bring an adapter unless travelling from UK, Ireland, Malaysia or Hong Kong.

travelTips.title: Getting around
travelTips.bodyHtml: MRT is fast, clean and air-conditioned – Bugis station sits right at the hotel doorstep. Buses cover the rest. For point-to-point rides, Grab and Gojek are the local equivalents of Uber – easy to use and widely available. Uber does not operate in Singapore.

travelTips.title: Weather in mid‑May
travelTips.body: Hot and humid, 26–32 °C (79–90 °F). Expect short afternoon thunderstorms. Pack light, breathable clothes, a small umbrella and sunscreen. Indoor venues are strongly air-conditioned – a light layer is useful.

sightseeing.name: Gardens by the Bay
sightseeing.desc: Supertree Grove evening light show, Flower Dome and Cloud Forest conservatories – Singapore’s most spectacular green landmark

sightseeing.name: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark
sightseeing.desc: Observation deck 57 floors up with the city skyline at your feet – the infinity pool view you’ve seen on every postcard

sightseeing.name: Singapore Botanic Gardens
sightseeing.desc: UNESCO World Heritage Site with lush rainforest walks, the National Orchid Garden and the iconic Bandstand lawn

sightseeing.name: Kampong Gelam
sightseeing.desc: Singapore’s Malay-Arab quarter: Sultan Mosque, Haji Lane boutiques, and some of the city’s best shawarma and murtabak

sightseeing.name: National Gallery Singapore
sightseeing.desc: The world’s largest public collection of Southeast Asian art, housed in two restored colonial buildings on St Andrew’s Road

sightseeing.name: Little India
sightseeing.desc: A riot of colour and scent: spice shops, flower garlands, the Tekka hawker centre and the ornate Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple

saturday.title: Optional Saturday Tour & Lunch
saturday.intro: This is the optional programme that you have registered for. If you haven’t registered and would like to join us, please reach out to

tour.title: Cultures of Singapore Sightseeing Tour
tour.includes: Private bus transportation, English-speaking guide and light refreshments
tour.spouses: Spouses are welcome if registered in advance
tour.description: Explore three of Singapore’s most iconic neighbourhoods on a guided small-bus tour. In Little India, soak up the buzz of roadside stalls, flower-garland artisans and gold-jewellery shops. In Chinatown, wander conserved shophouses that once housed coolies and secret societies, past a mosque, a Hindu temple and a Buddhist temple on the same street. In Kampong Gelam, admire the majestic Sultan Mosque, browse Arab Street’s textile shops and discover Haji Lane’s vibrant murals.

lunch.title: Group Lunch at Penang Place
lunch.description: Following the tour, join us for a casual group buffet lunch at Penang Place — one of Singapore’s best-loved Penang restaurants, famous for its Char Kway Teow, Assam Laksa and Beef Rendang.
lunch.note: Guests aged 60 and above receive a 20% discount.
lunch.afternoteHtml: After lunch, feel free to enjoy your afternoon exploring more of Singapore, browsing the countless shopping malls, or simply walking back to the Andaz hotel — it’s just a 12-minute walk.
